response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, seasoned senior engineer with genuine systems chops, but he misses the role's hardest gate: 8 years of C++. His C++ exposure amounts to a short stint at National Instruments and a thesis project, and C++ does not even appear in his skills list — his systems work is in DLang and his recent career is TypeScript/Node full-stack product engineering. Distributed-systems experience exists (low-latency micro-services at Sociomantic, Kubernetes-deployed GISCollective) but is neither sustained for 5 years nor at cloud-compute-platform scale, and his leadership record is mentoring via workshops rather than formally leading cloud or DevOps engineers on high-impact platform projects.
strengths: Low-latency distributed-systems experience (micro-services handling millions of HTTP requests per second, async I/O, caching)
Strong mentoring and knowledge-sharing record (workshops, multi-module courses, refactoring dojos, conference talks)
Broad, adaptable engineering background across web, mobile, embedded, and DevOps with solid craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD)
concerns: Falls far short of the required 8 years of C++; C++ is absent from his skills list and his systems language is DLang
Large-scale infrastructure and cloud experience is fragmented and well under the required 5 years of depth
No formal technical-leadership role owning platform strategy or leading cloud/DevOps engineers; recent work is full-stack product engineering

key_factors.factor: Limited cloud platform and infrastructure depth
